key_insight: A sparsity-promoting fine-tuning strategy can adapt E(3)-equivariant materials foundation models with near-full-performance while changing only a tiny fraction of parameters, and the resulting sparse updates can be physically interpretable.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical, lower-cost way to calibrate interatomic potential models for new molecular and crystalline domains without retraining the whole network, making deployment more efficient and explainable.
method: Selectively update parameters using sparsity-promoting fine-tuning rather than full fine-tuning.
Exploit the structural properties of E(3)-equivariant materials foundation models to preserve geometric consistency.
Use sparse update patterns to improve both calibration and interpretability.
evidence_signal: Across molecular and crystalline benchmarks, the method matches or exceeds full fine-tuning and equivariant low-rank adaptation while updating only about 3% of parameters, and sometimes as little as 0.5%; it also generalizes to magnetic moment and magnetism-aware energy prediction.
watch_item: Sparse adaptation may be sensitive to how the sparsity mask or regularization is chosen, so implementation details could affect stability and transferability across different materials tasks.
paper_title: Robust and Interpretable Adaptation of Equivariant Materials Foundation Models via Sparsity-promoting Fine-tuning
